title: 50 Rupees
description: Mahatma Gandhi, Lion capital of Asoka column.
body: The Reserve Bank of India introduced this 50 Rupee note on 18 August 2017 as part of the Mahatma Gandhi (New) Series, replacing the earlier design. New-date variants with updated governor signatures have continued to be issued, including a 2026-dated version. Fluorescent blue in color and measuring 135 × 66 mm, the obverse bears the portrait of Mahatma Gandhi, the Ashoka Pillar emblem, and micro-text security elements. The reverse depicts the Hampi with Chariot motif — the iconic stone chariot (Garuda Ratha) at the Vittala Temple complex, a UNESCO World Heritage Site in Karnataka — along with the Swachh Bharat Abhiyan logo and slogan.

highlights.reason: Part of the RBI's Mahatma Gandhi New Series introduced following the 2016 demonetisation; new-date variants update signatories while retaining the design.
highlights.design: Obverse: Mahatma Gandhi portrait, Ashoka Pillar emblem. Reverse: Hampi with Chariot (Garuda Ratha stone chariot, Vittala Temple, Karnataka); Swachh Bharat logo.
highlights.security: Windowed demetalized security thread with 'भारत' (Bharat) and 'RBI'; latent image of '50' on right vertical band; Mahatma Gandhi watermark and electrotype '50'; see-through register; intaglio printing; optically variable ink denomination; fluorescent fibers; Braille-like square tactile mark.

title: 200 Rupees
description: Mahatma Gandhi, Lion capital of Asoka column.
body: The Reserve Bank of India launched the 200 Rupee note on 25 August 2017 as a new denomination filling a gap in the Mahatma Gandhi (New) Series between ₹100 and ₹500. The bright yellow note measures 146 × 66 mm. The obverse features the portrait of Mahatma Gandhi alongside the Ashoka Pillar emblem and raised intaglio printing. The reverse depicts the Great Stupa of Sanchi in Madhya Pradesh — an iconic Buddhist monument dating to the 3rd century BCE and a UNESCO World Heritage Site — reflecting India's ancient cultural heritage. New-date variants bearing updated governor signatures, including a 2026 version, continue to circulate.

highlights.reason: New denomination introduced to fill the gap between ₹100 and ₹500 in the Mahatma Gandhi New Series following demonetisation in 2016.
highlights.design: Obverse: Mahatma Gandhi portrait, Ashoka Pillar emblem, raised intaglio text. Reverse: Great Stupa of Sanchi, Madhya Pradesh (3rd century BCE Buddhist monument).
highlights.security: Green-to-blue color-shifting windowed security thread with 'भारत' and 'RBI'; latent image of '200' on vertical band; see-through register with floral denomination design; Mahatma Gandhi and electrotype '200' watermarks; raised intaglio printing; angular bleed lines for tactile identification; optically variable ink '200' (green to blue).

title: 20.000 Rupiah
description: Dr. G. S. S. J. Ratulangi, arms, outline of Indonesia.
body: Bank Indonesia's 2022-series 20,000 Rupiah note was issued on 17 August 2022 to celebrate Indonesia's 77th Independence Day anniversary. A new-date variant bearing a 2026 date (B621e) has been confirmed, carrying updated signatures. The obverse features Dr. G.S.S.J. Ratulangi, a national independence hero and the first governor of Sulawesi Province, set against the Indonesian coat of arms, a black orchid, and a national map. The reverse depicts a Tari Gong dancer from East Kalimantan and a sea turtle off the Derawan Islands. The predominantly green-blue note (141 × 65 mm) forms part of the complete 2022 series which replaced the 2016 rupiah family.

highlights.reason: 2026-dated variant of the 2022 independence anniversary series, updating the circulating note with new signatures while retaining the existing design.
highlights.design: Obverse: Dr. G.S.S.J. Ratulangi portrait, Indonesian coat of arms, black orchid, map. Reverse: Tari Gong dancer from East Kalimantan, Derawan Islands sea turtle.
highlights.security: Windowed demetalized security thread 'BI 20000'; Oto Iskandar di Nata watermark; UV fluorescence; phosphorescent ink; infrared ink; magnetic security thread; intaglio printing.

title: 200 Naira
body: The Central Bank of Nigeria unveiled redesigned 200, 500, and 1,000 Naira notes in November 2022, with the updated 200 Naira entering circulation by 15 December 2022. Subsequent signature-change variants have continued through 2026. The 200 Naira note measures 151 × 78 mm and is distinctly rendered in red, pink, and orange — a significant shift from its previous muted green-brown palette. The obverse retains the portrait of Sir Ahmadu Bello, the Sardauna of Sokoto and a founding leader of Nigeria's First Republic. The reverse depicts agricultural commodities, livestock farming, and the Nigerian coat of arms, celebrating the country's agrarian economy.

highlights.reason: Redesigned in 2022 at President Buhari's approval to reduce counterfeiting and curb corruption by making existing notes harder to hoard; new-signature variants updated through 2026.
highlights.design: Obverse: Sir Ahmadu Bello (Sardauna of Sokoto) portrait. Reverse: Agricultural commodities, livestock, Nigerian coat of arms, yellow trumpet flowers.
highlights.security: Hand-engraved portrait; windowed metallic security thread with 'CBN' inscription; CBN 200 watermark; portrait watermark; optically variable ink (blue to green); embossed coat of arms gold patch; see-through register; raised intaglio printing; UV-fluorescent security ink.

title: 100 Kina
description: Arms, Parliament building (Port Moresby). First Anniversary of the Passing of Sir Julius Chan (2025-2026).
body: This is the regular circulation 100 Kina note issued by the Bank of Papua New Guinea bearing a 2026 date, distinct from the Sir Julius Chan commemorative issue. The standard 100 Kina has been the flagship denomination of Papua New Guinea's polymer note series for many years. The obverse features Parliament House in Port Moresby and the National Crest of Papua New Guinea — a bird of paradise perched on a Kundu drum with a ceremonial spear. The reverse carries traditional cultural imagery representing the diversity of Papua New Guinea's people, including canoes and traditional ceremonial elements. The note is printed on Guardian polymer substrate by Note Printing Australia.

highlights.reason: Standard annual circulation issue of the flagship 100 Kina denomination, updated with a 2026 date and current governor signatures.
highlights.design: Obverse: Parliament House, Port Moresby; National Crest (bird of paradise on Kundu drum). Reverse: Traditional Papua New Guinean cultural imagery including canoes and ceremonial elements.
highlights.security: Guardian polymer substrate; shadow-image watermark; intaglio printing; printed by Note Printing Australia.

title: 5 Pounds
body: The Government of Saint Helena issued this redesigned 5 Pound polymer note, which entered official circulation on 22 January 2026. It is the smallest denomination of a new three-note series (£5, £10, £20) that replaced the previous King Charles III paper series with more secure and durable polymer notes. Printed on polymer by De La Rue and measuring 135 × 70 mm, the blue note bears the official portrait of King Charles III on the obverse. The reverse depicts two of Saint Helena's most beloved natural symbols: the endemic Saint Helena Ebony flower (Trochetiopsis ebenus) and Jonathan the Tortoise, the world's oldest known living land animal at over 190 years of age.

highlights.reason: Transition from paper to polymer currency featuring King Charles III; modernises the island's banknotes while preserving Saint Helena's distinct identity through natural heritage imagery.
highlights.design: Obverse: Official portrait of King Charles III. Reverse: Saint Helena Ebony flower (Trochetiopsis ebenus) and Jonathan the Tortoise (Aldabrachelys gigantea hololissa).
highlights.security: De La Rue polymer substrate; security features intrinsic to polymer construction (transparency, durability, counterfeit resistance).

title: 10 Pounds
body: The 10 Pound polymer note from the Government of Saint Helena entered circulation on 22 January 2026 as part of the new series alongside the £5 and £20 issues. Measuring 135 × 70 mm, it is produced on polymer by De La Rue and is rendered in red. Like all notes in this series, the obverse carries the official effigy of King Charles III. The reverse mirrors the theme of the companion denominations, featuring the Saint Helena Ebony (Trochetiopsis ebenus) — a critically endangered endemic flowering tree — and Jonathan the Tortoise, the Seychelles giant tortoise resident at Plantation House who serves as a global symbol of Saint Helena.

highlights.reason: New polymer series featuring King Charles III, replacing earlier paper notes; polymer substrate significantly increases durability and security for the island's small-circulation currency.
highlights.design: Obverse: Official portrait of King Charles III. Reverse: Saint Helena Ebony flower (Trochetiopsis ebenus) and Jonathan the Tortoise.
highlights.security: De La Rue polymer substrate; security features inherent to polymer (transparent elements, anti-counterfeiting printing).

title: 20 Pounds
body: The highest denomination in Saint Helena's new 2026 polymer series, the 20 Pound note entered official circulation on 22 January 2026. De La Rue printed the 135 × 70 mm note on polymer in a brown color scheme. The obverse shows the official royal portrait of King Charles III. The reverse consistently features the same natural imagery as the £5 and £10 notes — the Saint Helena Ebony flower and Jonathan the Tortoise — creating a unified visual identity across the series. The notes are signed by Governor Richards, Financial Secretary Yon, and Treasurer O'Bey/Francis.

highlights.reason: Largest denomination of the new polymer series; designed to give Saint Helena's currency a modern, durable format bearing the new monarch's portrait.
highlights.design: Obverse: Official portrait of King Charles III. Reverse: Saint Helena Ebony flower (Trochetiopsis ebenus) and Jonathan the Tortoise.
highlights.security: De La Rue polymer substrate; polymer-inherent anti-counterfeiting properties.

title: 50 Dirhams
description: Seven founding fathers with flag after signing the union document.
body: This is the 2026 new-date variant of the UAE's polymer 50 Dirhams note, originally introduced on 7 December 2021 as the UAE's Golden Jubilee commemorative issue and its first polymer banknote. The design remains unchanged from the 2021 issue: the 151 × 64 mm note is produced on De La Rue SAFEGUARD® polymer in violet and blue tones. The obverse depicts Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan, Wahat Al Karama, and the seven founding fathers. The reverse shows Sheikh Zayed signing the 1971 union charter at the Etihad Museum. Braille embossing, transparent windows with optically variable imagery, and advanced intaglio printing provide multi-layer authentication.

highlights.reason: Updated circulation variant of the 2021 Golden Jubilee polymer 50 Dirhams, reflecting standard practice of issuing new-date or new-signature versions while retaining the commemorative design.
highlights.design: Obverse: Sheikh Zayed portrait, Wahat Al Karama (Martyrs' Memorial), seven founding fathers. Reverse: Sheikh Zayed signing union charter, Etihad Museum.
highlights.security: De La Rue SAFEGUARD® polymer; transparent windows; fluorescent blue UAE nation brand marks; advanced intaglio printing; Braille embossing; denomination-specific color-shifting optically variable features.

title: 10 ZiG (Zimbabwe Gold)
description: Cape buffalo, Chiremba balancing rock formation.
body: The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe introduced the upgraded ZiG 10 note on 7 April 2026 as part of the new 'Big 5' ZiG series, replacing the original 2024 ZiG notes that had suffered rapid wear. The Zimbabwe Gold (ZiG) currency, launched in April 2024 and linked to gold reserves, required a more durable redesign. The ZiG 10 features an African Buffalo (Cape buffalo) rendered in intaglio on the obverse, alongside the RBZ's three balancing rocks logo. The reverse depicts the Matopo Hills — the ancient granite domes and balancing rocks of Matobo National Park in southern Zimbabwe, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Security is enhanced over the 2024 series.

highlights.reason: Redesigned 'Big 5' upgrade of the original 2024 ZiG notes; banks and retailers had reported rapid fading and tearing; the RBZ issued a more durable note with enhanced security for the ZiG currency backed by gold and foreign reserves.
highlights.design: Obverse: African Buffalo (Cape buffalo), RBZ three balancing rocks logo. Reverse: Matopo Hills (Matobo National Park), Zimbabwe.
highlights.security: Intaglio-printed wildlife with tactile relief; magnetic security thread with denomination inscription; PEAK™ anti-copy element; SPARK® optical variable device showing Zimbabwe Bird; ColorShift™ element; see-through register; watermark of Zimbabwe Bird with denomination; tactile horizontal lines for visually impaired.

title: 20 ZiG (Zimbabwe Gold)
body: The ZiG 20 entered circulation on 7 April 2026 as the middle denomination of Zimbabwe's new 'Big 5' banknote series. The upgrade was prompted by widespread durability failures in the original 2024 ZiG notes. The obverse of the ZiG 20 features an African Elephant — the largest of the Big Five — rendered in high-relief intaglio printing, alongside the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe's logo of three balancing rocks. The reverse depicts the Parliament of Zimbabwe building in Harare, linking Zimbabwe's wildlife heritage with its constitutional institutions. The note carries a full suite of modern security features including SPARK® optical variable device and PEAK™ anti-copy elements.

highlights.reason: Upgraded Big 5 redesign of the 2024 ZiG 20 note; addresses documented durability problems and introduces enhanced security features for the gold-backed ZiG currency.
highlights.design: Obverse: African Elephant, RBZ three balancing rocks logo. Reverse: Parliament of Zimbabwe, Harare.
highlights.security: Intaglio-printed elephant with tactile surface; magnetic security thread; PEAK™ anti-copy element; SPARK® optical variable Zimbabwe Bird; ColorShift™ device; see-through register; Zimbabwe Bird watermark with denomination; tactile lines for visually impaired.

title: 50 ZiG (Zimbabwe Gold)
body: The highest of the three initial 'Big 5' ZiG denominations launched on 7 April 2026, the ZiG 50 completes the first phase of Zimbabwe's upgraded gold-backed banknote series. The obverse depicts a Black Rhinoceros — one of Africa's most endangered mammals and a symbol of Zimbabwe's conservation efforts — alongside the RBZ logo. The reverse features a national landmark as published in the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe's official gazette. The ZiG 50 carries the most comprehensive security package of the three initial denominations. Higher denominations (ZiG 100 featuring a Leopard and ZiG 200 featuring a Lion) are planned for later issuance.

highlights.reason: Highest-value note in the first tranche of the Big 5 ZiG series; part of the broader upgrade program addressing durability failures of the original 2024 ZiG notes and reinforcing confidence in Zimbabwe's gold-backed currency.
highlights.design: Obverse: Black Rhinoceros, RBZ three balancing rocks logo. Reverse: National landmark as gazetted by the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe.
highlights.security: Intaglio-printed rhinoceros with tactile relief; magnetic security thread with denomination inscription; PEAK™ anti-copy element; SPARK® optical variable Zimbabwe Bird device; ColorShift™ element; see-through register; Zimbabwe Bird watermark; tactile lines and denomination-specific accessibility markers.
